title: Adversarial split and purged sequence validation
summary: The train/test ids are contiguous and the task is sequential, so random CV can reward features that exploit race-order artifacts or future-adjacent rows.
rationale: The train/test ids are contiguous and the task is sequential, so random CV can reward features that exploit race-order artifacts or future-adjacent rows. Search results on time-series CV emphasize chronological splits and gaps to prevent future leakage.
implementation_hint: Build a train-vs-test adversarial classifier using only original and engineered features. Use its high-importance shift features to define validation folds: hold out later ids/years/race-years, purge neighboring laps within the same driver-race sequence, and compare against current CV before trusting new features.
expected_effect: Should improve public-LB reliability by selecting features/models that survive realistic sequence and distribution shift, even if local CV drops slightly.
risk: May reduce local CV and slow iteration; the adversarial model must not be used to overfit public test distribution directly.
